title: larix japonica
intro: Larix japonica, also known as the Japanese larch, is a deciduous coniferous tree native to East Asia. The bark of the Larix japonica plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion. Herbal preparations of Larix japonica include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
